results.claim: Validator independently tested 21 cases with additional NaN/Inf and CUDA graph checks
Cross-check between champion and validator kernel benchmarks showed <1% delta
Validator benchmarked both paths under CUDA graph capture independently, found 1.11-1.25x warm speedup
Validator flagged champion's 5.5x speedup claim as inflated by eager-mode benchmark
Validator measured 3.4x regression under CUDA graphs, contradicting champion's 1.10x claim
Validator identified that champion benchmark excluded CUDA graphs, inflating Triton baseline by ~25us Python overhead
Campaign exhausted after 3 rounds with cumulative 1.053x E2E speedup
Only op_w8a8_tiny_fp8_gemm was shipped
op_w8a8_tiny_fp8_gemm achieved 13.7% BS=1, 5.3% BS=8, 0.7% BS=32 E2E improvement
bottleneck_analysis.md used near-optimal framing for fused_moe_kernel at 79.6% BW, below the 85% anti-pattern threshold
Champion-3 explicitly invoked near-optimal framing to justify pivoting away from fused_moe
Debate summary reinforced near-optimal framing, discouraging fused_moe optimization in subsequent rounds
Champion-3 projected 1.69% conservative E2E improvement at BS=8
Actual E2E improvement for op_fused_moe_route_prep was 0.65% at BS=8, 2.6-3.4x below conservative projection
Corrected E2E drops from 1.49% to 0.41% for prototyped 3-op fusion due to baseline provenance failure
Delegate-1a identified the no-L40S-w8a8-config gap that enabled op_w8a8_tiny_fp8_gemm
Delegate-2a audited champion-3 prototype and identified production dispatch path discrepancy
BLOCK_N=256 forces num_stages=2, yielding ~0% net E2E due to 3.6% regression
Champion's benchmark without CUDA graphs was misleading; validator caught the regression
Amdahl analysis showed f=0.013 gives ceiling of 0.16% E2E, explaining negligible impact
Only 10-45% of kernel-level savings materialized as E2E due to NCCL absorption
Bottleneck analysis concluded 'No red flags' and 'near-optimal for its configuration' at 79.6% BW
Config-only optimization replaced BLOCK_SIZE_M=64 with BLOCK_SIZE_M=16, reducing register pressure 2.5x
E2E sweep showed 13.7% at BS=1, 5.3% at BS=8, 0.7% at BS=32
Root cause: FP8->FP16 conversion approach caused 4.4x instruction overhead on SM89
ncu showed WarpSpec has 4.4x more instructions and 21% lower BW utilization than Triton
Production dispatch uses topkGating with fused sigmoid, not separate torch.topk + int32 cast
NCCL spin-wait absorption reduces kernel-to-E2E translation ratio to 10-45%
Delegate-1a identified production config BLOCK_SIZE_M=16, num_stages=3 for all batch sizes
BLOCK_N=256 and num_stages=3 mutually exclusive on L40S; DeepGEMM/CUTLASS blocked
DeepGEMM blocked on L40S (SM89, requires SM90+); CUTLASS block FP8 requires SM90+
Model has 256 experts, top_k=8, GatedDeltaNet linear attention, and very small per-expert GEMMs
op_w8a8_tiny_fp8_gemm passed Gate 5.3b, but two validator_gate53 directories indicate a sweep retry
Validation report references kernel_proof_w8a8 for logs while multiple kernel_proof variants exist
Dispatch guard was in wrong function -- kernel never dispatched in production
Champion-3 claimed 120 us/step from inter-kernel gap savings under CUDA graphs
Champion-3's own CUDA graph experiment showed 0 us inter-kernel overhead, contradicting 120 us claim
Champion-3 projected 1.69-2.18% E2E based on inflated baseline
Production uses topkGating (sigmoid fused in, int32 pre-allocated), not torch.topk + int32 cast
BLOCK_N=256 forces num_stages=2, causing 4.6% pipeline regression
Champion benchmark excluded CUDA graphs; Triton measurement included ~25us Python overhead
Under CUDA graphs WarpSpec is 3.4x slower than Triton
